drugs.notes: Cycles 1, 3, 5 only (Maxi-CHOP cycles). Intravenous infusion in 250ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 30 minutes or as an intravenous bolus over 10 minutes. Dose banded per national dose bands (20mg/ml).

Cycles 1, 3, 5 only (Maxi-CHOP cycles). Intravenous bolus over 10 minutes. Dose banded per national dose bands (2mg/ml). Maximum lifetime cumulative dose 450mg/m² (400mg/m² if prior mediastinal/pericardial radiotherapy). Discontinue if cardiac failure develops.

Cycles 1, 3, 5 only (Maxi-CHOP cycles). Intravenous bolus in 50ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 10 minutes. Flat dose 2mg (maximum). Dose banded per national dose bands (1mg/ml) when dose reductions are necessary.
Cycles 1, 3, 5 only (Maxi-CHOP cycles). Taken in the morning with or after food. Administration begins on the morning of chemotherapy.

All 6 cycles. Intravenous infusion in 500ml sodium chloride 0.9% (cycles 1, 3, 5 day 1; cycles 2, 4 day 1; cycle 6 days 1 and 9 - day 9 dose in 500ml). Cycle 6 also given on day 9 as outpatient. Dose rounded to nearest 100mg. Rate of administration varies - refer to rituximab administration guidelines. Consider omitting on cycle 1 if WBC >=25x10^9/L.

Cycles 2, 4, 6 only (high-dose cytarabine cycles). Given every 12 hours (4 doses total over days 1 and 2). Intravenous infusion in 1000ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 180 minutes. Doses MUST be given 12 hours apart. Dose banded per national dose bands (100mg/ml).

dose_modifications.condition: Haematological: neutrophils <1x10^9/L or platelets <75x10^9/L on day 1
dose_modifications.notes: Delay treatment until neutrophils >=1x10^9/L and platelets >=75x10^9/L. No dose reductions for haematological toxicity - delay only.

dose_modifications.condition: Hepatic impairment: Bilirubin >30 µmol/L or AST/ALT 2-3xULN
dose_modifications.notes: Clinical decision. Evidence that exposure to active metabolites may not be increased, suggesting dose reduction may not be necessary.

dose_modifications.condition: Hepatic impairment: Bilirubin >40 µmol/L
Hepatic impairment: Bilirubin <30 µmol/L and AST/ALT 2-3xULN
Hepatic impairment: Bilirubin 30-50 µmol/L and/or AST/ALT >3xULN
Hepatic impairment: Bilirubin 51-85 µmol/L
Hepatic impairment: Bilirubin >85 µmol/L
Hepatic impairment: any
Hepatic impairment: Bilirubin 30-51 µmol/L or AST/ALT 60-180 units
Hepatic impairment: Bilirubin >51 µmol/L and AST/ALT normal
Hepatic impairment: Bilirubin >51 µmol/L and AST/ALT >180 units
Renal impairment: CrCl >20 ml/min
dose_modifications.notes: Full dose. Consider mesna in patients with pre-existing bladder disorders.

dose_modifications.condition: Renal impairment: CrCl 10-20 ml/min
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce to 75% of original dose. Consider mesna (oral 40% of cyclophosphamide dose at 0, 2 and 6 hours).

dose_modifications.condition: Renal impairment: CrCl <10 ml/min
Renal impairment: any
Renal impairment: CrCl <10 ml/min
dose_modifications.notes: Consider dose reduction in severe renal failure (clinical decision).

dose_modifications.condition: Renal impairment: any
Renal impairment: any
Neurotoxicity: NCI-CTC grade 2 motor or grade 3 sensory neurological toxicity
Neurotoxicity: higher than grade 2 motor or grade 3 sensory, or toxicity increases despite dose reduction
Cardiac failure develops

pre_medications: Chlorphenamine 10mg IV bolus (30 minutes prior to rituximab; cycles 2, 4, 6 hydrocortisone also)
Hydrocortisone 100mg IV bolus (cycles 2, 4, 6 only - 30 minutes prior to rituximab)
Paracetamol 1000mg oral (30 minutes prior to rituximab)
Prednisolone 100mg oral on morning of treatment (cycles 3 and 5 - self-administered, also serves as rituximab pre-medication)
Ondansetron 8mg oral or IV (15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy)
Dexamethasone 4mg oral twice daily for 3 days (cycles 2, 4, 6 only antiemetic)
Metoclopramide 10mg oral three times daily when required
Ondansetron 8mg oral twice daily for 3 days (take home)
Allopurinol 300mg oral once daily for 7 days (cycle 1 only)
Corticosteroid eye drops: prednisolone 0.5% or dexamethasone 0.1% - 1 drop both eyes four times daily for 4 days with cytarabine administration
Aciclovir 400mg oral twice daily (anti-infective prophylaxis)
Co-trimoxazole 960mg oral once daily Monday, Wednesday and Friday (cycles 1-5)
Pentamidine 300mg nebuliser once monthly from cycle 6 only, continued for 6 months after completion or per CD4 count

Filgrastim (or bioequivalent) 30 million units subcutaneous once daily from day 8 (or lenograstim 33.6 million units subcutaneous once daily from day 8) following each cycle until neutrophil count >1x10^9/L
special_warnings: There are multiple versions of this protocol. Choice depends on patient age - Nordic-2000 is for patients aged 60 years and above.
CD20 positive Mantle Cell Lymphoma only. Performance status 0 or 1.

Alternating regimen: odd cycles (1,3,5) are Maxi-CHOP plus rituximab (outpatient); even cycles (2,4,6) are high-dose cytarabine 2000mg/m² plus rituximab (inpatient). Cycle 6 rituximab also given on day 9.
Consider omitting rituximab on cycle 1 if white cell count >=25x10^9/L - seek consultant advice.
Cytarabine is given TWICE a day (12 hours apart) - 4 doses total over 2 days.
Corticosteroid eye drops are mandatory with cytarabine administration to reduce conjunctivitis risk.
Baseline LVEF should be measured in patients with history of cardiac problems, cardiac risk factors or elderly. Discontinue doxorubicin if cardiac failure develops.
Maximum lifetime cumulative doxorubicin dose 450mg/m² (400mg/m² if prior mediastinal/pericardial radiotherapy).
Maximum vincristine dose is 2mg (flat dose).
Check hepatitis B status before starting rituximab. Monitor for PML during rituximab therapy.
Rituximab infusion-related reactions: cytokine release syndrome risk; have emergency medications available.
CMV serology, LDH, beta-2 microglobulin, DAT and immunoglobulin levels required at baseline.
NPSA/2008/RRR04 must be followed for IV vincristine administration.
In principle, dose reductions should not be re-escalated without consultant approval. If third dose reduction required, stop treatment.

drugs.notes: Cycles 1, 3, 5 only (Maxi-CHOP cycles). Intravenous infusion in 250ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 30 minutes or as an intravenous bolus over 10 minutes. Dose banded per national dose bands (20mg/ml).

Cycles 1, 3, 5 only (Maxi-CHOP cycles). Intravenous bolus over 10 minutes. Dose banded per national dose bands (2mg/ml). Maximum lifetime cumulative dose 450mg/m² (400mg/m² if prior mediastinal/pericardial radiotherapy). Discontinue if cardiac failure develops.

Cycles 1, 3, 5 only (Maxi-CHOP cycles). Intravenous bolus in 50ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 10 minutes. Flat dose 2mg (maximum). Dose banded per national dose bands (1mg/ml) when dose reductions are necessary.
Cycles 1, 3, 5 only (Maxi-CHOP cycles). Taken in the morning with or after food. Administration begins on the morning of chemotherapy.

All 6 cycles. Intravenous infusion in 500ml sodium chloride 0.9% (cycles 1, 3, 5 day 1; cycles 2, 4 day 1; cycle 6 days 1 and 9 - day 9 dose in 500ml). Cycle 6 also given on day 9 as outpatient. Dose rounded to nearest 100mg. Rate of administration varies - refer to rituximab administration guidelines. Consider omitting on cycle 1 if WBC >=25x10^9/L.

Cycles 2, 4, 6 only (high-dose cytarabine cycles). Given every 12 hours (4 doses total over days 1 and 2). Intravenous infusion in 1000ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 180 minutes. Doses MUST be given 12 hours apart. Dose banded per national dose bands (100mg/ml).

special_warnings: There are multiple versions of this protocol. Choice depends on patient age - Nordic-3000 is for patients aged 59 years and below.
CD20 positive Mantle Cell Lymphoma only. Performance status 0 or 1.

Alternating regimen: odd cycles (1,3,5) are Maxi-CHOP plus rituximab (outpatient); even cycles (2,4,6) are high-dose cytarabine 3000mg/m² plus rituximab (inpatient). Cycle 6 rituximab also given on day 9.
Consider omitting rituximab on cycle 1 if white cell count >=25x10^9/L - seek consultant advice.
Cytarabine is given TWICE a day (12 hours apart) - 4 doses total over 2 days.
Corticosteroid eye drops are MANDATORY with cytarabine 3000mg/m² administration to reduce conjunctivitis risk.
Baseline LVEF should be measured in patients with history of cardiac problems, cardiac risk factors or elderly. Discontinue doxorubicin if cardiac failure develops.
Maximum lifetime cumulative doxorubicin dose 450mg/m² (400mg/m² if prior mediastinal/pericardial radiotherapy).

In principle, dose reductions should not be re-escalated without consultant approval. If third dose reduction required, stop treatment.

drugs.notes: Cycle 1: given on days 1, 8, and 15 (1000mg each occasion) in 250ml sodium chloride 0.9%. Cycles 2-6: day 1 only (1000mg). Cycles 7-8: day 1 only (obinutuzumab monotherapy - no CHOP). Flat dose 1000mg (not weight/BSA based). NOT dose-reducible based on haematological parameters. Followed by maintenance obinutuzumab as separate protocol. Infusion rate: cycle 1 day 1 start at 50mg/hr, escalate by 50mg/hr every 30 min to max 400mg/hr; subsequent infusions per protocol guidelines.

Cycles 1-6 only. Intravenous bolus over 10 minutes. Dose banded per national dose bands (20mg/ml). Consider mesna in patients with pre-existing bladder disorders (oral 40% cyclophosphamide dose at 0, 2 and 6 hours).

Cycles 1-6 only. Intravenous bolus over 10 minutes. Dose banded per national dose bands (2mg/ml). Maximum lifetime cumulative dose 450mg/m² (400mg/m² if prior mediastinal/pericardial radiotherapy). Discontinue if cardiac failure develops.
Cycles 1-6 only. Intravenous bolus in 50ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 10 minutes. Maximum dose 2mg. Dose banded per national dose bands (1mg/ml).
Cycles 1-6 only. Taken on days 2, 3, 4, 5 (100mg once daily in the morning). Methylprednisolone is given as obinutuzumab pre-medication on day 1.

dose_modifications.condition: Haematological: neutrophils <1x10^9/L on day 1 (unless disease-related)
dose_modifications.notes: Delay therapy until neutrophils >=1x10^9/L. Consider growth factors as secondary prophylaxis. Reconsider treatment if not recovered after 14 days.

dose_modifications.condition: Haematological: Grade 4 neutropenia or any febrile neutropenia following any cycle
dose_modifications.notes: Give growth factors with all subsequent cycles.

dose_modifications.condition: Haematological: Grade 4 neutropenia leading to infection despite G-CSF support
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce dose of cyclophosphamide and doxorubicin by 50% for all subsequent cycles.

dose_modifications.condition: Haematological: Grade 4 neutropenia recurs despite 50% dose reduction in cyclophosphamide and doxorubicin
Haematological: platelets <100x10^9/L on day 1 (unless disease-related)
dose_modifications.notes: Delay therapy until platelets >=100x10^9/L. Reconsider treatment if not recovered after 14 days.

dose_modifications.condition: Haematological: Grade 4 thrombocytopenia following any cycle
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce dose by 50% for all subsequent cycles.

dose_modifications.condition: Haematological: Grade 4 thrombocytopenia recurs despite 50% dose reduction
Haematological: any
dose_modifications.notes: There are no dose reductions for obinutuzumab based on haematological parameters.

dose_modifications.condition: Hepatic impairment: any
dose_modifications.notes: Evidence suggests dose modification not necessary.

dose_modifications.condition: Hepatic impairment: Bilirubin <30 µmol/L and AST/ALT 2-3xULN
Hepatic impairment: Bilirubin 30-50 µmol/L and/or AST/ALT >3xULN
Hepatic impairment: Bilirubin 51-85 µmol/L
Hepatic impairment: Bilirubin >85 µmol/L
Hepatic impairment: Bilirubin 30-51 µmol/L or AST/ALT 60-180 units/L
Hepatic impairment: Bilirubin >51 µmol/L and AST/ALT normal
Hepatic impairment: Bilirubin >51 µmol/L and AST/ALT >180 units/L
Renal impairment: CrCl >20 ml/min
Renal impairment: CrCl 10-20 ml/min
Renal impairment: CrCl <10 ml/min
Renal impairment: CrCl <10 ml/min
dose_modifications.notes: Consider dose reduction in severe renal failure (clinical decision).

dose_modifications.condition: Renal impairment: mild to moderate
dose_modifications.notes: Dose adjustment not considered necessary for obinutuzumab in mild to moderate renal impairment.

dose_modifications.condition: Renal impairment: any
Obinutuzumab toxicity: Grade 2 or 3 related organ/non-haematological toxicity
dose_modifications.notes: Hold obinutuzumab until toxicity resolves to grade 1 or below.

dose_modifications.condition: Obinutuzumab toxicity: Grade 2 non-haematological toxicity delaying treatment by more than 4 weeks
Obinutuzumab toxicity: Grade 4 related organ/non-haematological toxicity, severe haemorrhage, severe skin reaction, pneumonitis, severe arrhythmias or other severe cardiovascular events
Obinutuzumab toxicity: Viral hepatitis or other serious infections; reactivation of hepatitis B
Non-haematological NCI-CTC grade 3 and above toxicity (general)
dose_modifications.notes: Delay until resolved to grade 2 or below, then reduce to 75%. If recurs, delay until recovery then reduce to 50% or discontinue.

dose_modifications.condition: Neurotoxicity: NCI-CTC grade 2 motor or grade 3 sensory neurological toxicity
Neurotoxicity: higher than grade 2 motor or grade 3 sensory, or toxicity increases despite dose reduction
Cardiac failure develops

pre_medications: Sodium chloride 0.9% 500ml IV infusion over 60 minutes (prior to obinutuzumab, cycle 1 day 1)

Methylprednisolone sodium succinate 80mg IV (60 minutes prior to obinutuzumab; cycle 1 day 1 all patients; cycle 1 days 8 and 15 and cycles 2-6 only if grade 3 infusion reaction history or lymphocyte count >25x10^9/L)
Chlorphenamine 10mg IV (60 minutes prior to obinutuzumab; cycle 1 day 1 all patients; cycle 1 days 8 and 15 and cycles 2-6 for patients with grades 1-2 or grade 3 infusion reaction history)
Paracetamol 1000mg oral (60 minutes prior to obinutuzumab; all patients cycle 1 day 1 and cycles 1 days 8 and 15 and all subsequent cycles)
Ondansetron 8mg oral or IV (15-30 minutes prior to CHOP on day 1)
Metoclopramide 10mg oral three times daily when required (take home)
Ondansetron 8mg oral twice daily for 3 days (take home)
Allopurinol 300mg oral once daily for 7 days (TLS prophylaxis for cycle 1 where risk indicated; high risk patients may require rasburicase)
Aciclovir 400mg oral twice daily (anti-infective prophylaxis, high risk patients)
Co-trimoxazole 960mg oral once daily Monday, Wednesday and Friday (anti-infective prophylaxis, high risk patients)
special_warnings: Obinutuzumab in combination with CHOP for untreated advanced follicular lymphoma (FLIPI score 2 or more). WHO Performance Status 0, 1, 2.
Obinutuzumab is NOT dose-reducible based on haematological parameters.
Obinutuzumab dose is a flat 1000mg dose (not BSA-based). Cycle 1 given on days 1, 8, and 15; cycles 2-6 on day 1 only; cycles 7-8 obinutuzumab monotherapy only.
Followed by obinutuzumab maintenance therapy as a separate protocol.
Infusion-related reactions are particularly common during cycle 1. Patients with high tumour burden or lymphocyte count >25x10^9/L at increased risk.
Withhold antihypertensive treatments for 12 hours prior to and throughout each obinutuzumab infusion and for the first hour after administration.
Tumour lysis syndrome risk: patients with high tumour burden and/or lymphocyte count >25x10^9/L and/or CrCl <70 ml/min should receive TLS prophylaxis.
Progressive multifocal leukoencephalopathy (PML) has been reported with obinutuzumab. Refer to neurologist if suspected.
Baseline LVEF required. Discontinue doxorubicin if cardiac failure develops.
Maximum lifetime cumulative doxorubicin dose 450mg/m² (400mg/m² if prior mediastinal/pericardial radiotherapy).
Maximum vincristine dose is 2mg.
FBC, U&Es and LFTs on day 1 of first six cycles; optionally days 8 and 15 of cycle 1.
NPSA/2008/RRR04 must be followed for IV vincristine administration.

Anti-CD20 therapy: HBV reactivation risk — screen all patients for HBsAg and HBcAb before starting. HBsAg positive: antiviral prophylaxis mandatory (entecavir or tenofovir). HBcAb positive/HBsAg negative: prophylaxis or close monitoring per local hepatology policy.

Flat dose 1000mg (not weight/BSA based). NOT dose-reducible based on haematological parameters. Followed by maintenance obinutuzumab as separate protocol. Infusion rate: cycle 1 day 1 start at 50mg/hr, escalate by 50mg/hr every 30 min to max 400mg/hr; subsequent infusions per protocol guidelines.
